name: Baba's Deli
address: 295 Bay St, Port Melbourne VIC 3207, Australia
comment:
Haven't had such a banging deli sandwich for a while. Grab some friends and come down to try all the flavours. These pics are across 2 visits in the same month.

The must get is defs the Mama's Vodka ($21) - Crumbed chicken, vodka sauce, parmesan lemon rocket & stracciatella cheese. It has a fatttt piece of chicken and pairs so well with the vodka chicken. We did try the special where it was in the fried pizza dough but prefer the focaccia, since the bread is so nice and fluffy.

The Pastrami ($23) was delicious as well and my friend loved her Spicy Tuna Melt. The Salami ($19) with spicy romesco sauce, herbed garlic mayo, pickled sumac onions & lemon parmesan rocket was nice and fresh but wasn't as wow factor as the chicken.

Service was lively and friendly and they really try to have a chat with all the patrons so it is a very inviting vibe.

name: Chubby Toast
address: 7 Claremont St, South Yarra VIC 3141, Australia
comment:
🔥🔥🔥 Highly recommended!! 🔥🔥🔥
Don’t hesitate—just come in, order, and try it!

I really love the sandwiches and the Thai Tea Brûlée.
If you’re a Thai tea fan, you cannot miss this menu.

This is a new shop (opened less than a month), and the staff are very nice and friendly. Please leave them honest reviews—they genuinely want to improve, and I love their open mindset and motivation.

I decided to visit because my friend brought me the Spicy Prawn Sandwich for lunch, but I only had time to eat it at 9 PM. Of course, the bread was already cold, as expected 😅 but I was so surprised that the filling was still delicious! That impressed me so much that I really wanted to try everything fresh.

Today (6 Dec 2025), I tried the Wagyu Teriyaki, and I’m so happy with my choice.
The only sad part is… now I want to come back and try all the flavours! 🥹✨
@enjoyhappylife.ig

name: Hi Fi By Terror Twilight
address: 316 Smith St, Collingwood VIC 3066, Australia
comment:
Absolutely obsessed with Hi-Fi. The coffee and sandwiches are genuinely stellar,  like, plan-your-morning-around-it good 😋. Completely worth the hype. I love that they’re always switching up the menu to keep things fresh, so there’s constantly something new to try… but honestly, the classics are so bloody good it’s easy to fall into a delicious routine. I’ve officially become a full-blown toastie slut and I’m not even trying to recover.

But above all else, it’s the service that keeps us coming back. The banter with Eimear is always golden. Even on days when I’m not grabbing anything, I still pop in just to say hello. She’s an absolute legend and such a credit to this place. Warm, funny, welcoming, the kind of place that feels like a neighbourhood institution in the making. If you’re in Collingwood and not going to Hi-Fi, you’re doing it wrong. ☕🥪✨

comment: Just had my sisters birthday celebration at Sloppy Joes and it was amazing! the food the service was on point and would like to shout out to Adam and his team who catered to us, he was a pure gentleman and would definitely come back! Unlimited chicken (tasted so good and not dry with other flavours), free ice-cream and donuts who could ask for more!

name: Sloppy Joe's
address: Shop 2/190 Queen St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia
comment:
Sloppy Joe’s by day, Floppy Joe’s by night — and honestly, both sides deliver.

We came in as a group of 7 (including 4 kids), and it handled us effortlessly. The all-you-can-eat fried chicken for $30 is an absolute steal, and the food itself, Next level. The chicken was incredible, especially the hot honey — that perfect mix of sweet heat that keeps you going back for more. Dangerous in the best way.

The sides were just as impressive and came out faster than you can say “thank you.” No waiting around, no chasing staff — just smooth, efficient service all night.

Speaking of service, it was spot on. Polite, friendly, and genuinely accommodating, especially with a bigger group and kids. You can tell they actually care about the experience, not just turning tables.

The place itself was clean, well-kept, and had a great energy to it. The vibe is relaxed and laid-back, but still lively enough to feel like you’re out somewhere worth being. Easy, comfortable, no pressure — just good food, good company, and a space that lets you enjoy both.

Highly recommend this spot — whether you’re going for a casual feed, bringing the family, or lining your stomach properly. We’ll definitely be back.

name: Smith + Deli
address: 107 Cambridge St, Collingwood VIC 3066, Australia
comment:
What amazing vegan options! I had the frittata, made from silken tofu with potato and some seasonal vegetables, topped with vegan pesto. Incredibly tasty and filling! My table companions had the tortilla (made from chickpea flour and potato) and the Dawn of the Veg sandwich. I tasted a bit of both, and they were very tasty. The presentation of the tortilla as a sort of muffin was particularly original.

However, I cannot give 5 stars. The chai latte was not top-notch. Watery, lacking flavor, and completely lacking a layer of foam. It was also very small for 6 AUD; apparently, a large one was a dollar more.

name: Smith + Deli
address: 107 Cambridge St, Collingwood VIC 3066, Australia
comment: Ordered a cake from here. Was mostly butter cream and barely any cake. Would not reccommend this place to anyone.

name: Smith + Deli
address: 107 Cambridge St, Collingwood VIC 3066, Australia
comment:
Former Melburnian here, now living in Sydney since 2019—and one thing I’ll always say is that Melbourne really knows how to appreciate a good thing and do VEGAN so well. I’ve visited Smith & Deli a number of times over the years whenever I’m back, and I’m consistently impressed by both the menu and the quality on offer.

This visit, we enjoyed a couple of sandwiches which were tasty and stocked up on some groceries but the absolute standout was the ready-meal shepherd’s pie we grabbed for a road trip and heated up for dinner—it was exceptional. I enjoyed it so much that I’m already planning a stop on the way back just to stock up on a few frozen meals to take home to Sydney.

name: Smith + Deli
address: 107 Cambridge St, Collingwood VIC 3066, Australia
comment: You cannot be vegan in Melbourne and not come here! It’s the best vegan bakery around! The almond danish is A MUST HAVE. The croissants and almond croissants are also soooo good! The coffee is always delicious which is a rare thing in Melbourne the last few years. I love the atmosphere and the staff is always so kind! The sandwiches are also really good but… croissants first!

name: Smith + Deli
address: 107 Cambridge St, Collingwood VIC 3066, Australia
comment: Waited 30 minutes for the staff to toast a sandwich that was already made, which they failed to do - ended up just getting a refund and left. The service here has gotten really terrible!

comment: Got the pulled beef sandwhich which i assume is supposed to be like rendang but honestly the sandwhich was pretty one dimensional and it got heavy quick. Definitely doesn’t mean it’s bad, still good and had a decent flavour, doesn’t really remind me of rendang tho (as a person who grew up eating rendang), i just wished it was less on the sweet side and more on the fragrant side. But i understand it might be adjusted for local taste buds and an introduction to rendang fusion. The crispy onion on top definitely added a good touch. My friend got the matcha and it was a skip for us, tasted almost like those instant matcha powders.

name: Warung Coffee
address: 667 Chapel St, South Yarra VIC 3141, Australia
comment:
Great sandwiches! The beef rendang sandwich could have more filling and I’d prefer the fried shallots IN the sandwich instead of outside.
The fish sandwich was 3/5 as the bun:fish ratio had more bun than fish.
Would definitely return to try more on the menu.

name: pip's deli

comment: from the food and coffee to the customer service and environment, it was all so amazing!! we got the mushroom melt - we’ve been to many deli places before but the bread here was to die for and a plus is the cute stickers!

name: pip's deli
address: 348 Chapel St, South Yarra VIC 3141, Australia
comment: The Chicken Waldorf is seriously delicious. It's not salty and is perfect for a low-calorie lunch. The combination of celery, nuts, apples, and that amazing toasted focaccia is so fresh and satisfying. Great choice if you want something light but filling.

name: pip's deli
address: 348 Chapel St, South Yarra VIC 3141, Australia
comment:
Recently visited this place and wanted to give it a try. I ordered the mushroom melt and the mango passionfruit iced espresso . The espresso was something new, really refreshing, and I truly enjoyed it. The mushroom melt was pretty standard but still tasty.

The service and staff were amazing.  Definitely worth checking out! 😊

name: pip's deli

comment: The beef is rich and flavorful. The sauce is fragrant and a little spicy, but it’s totally manageable.

comment: I ordered the chicken Waldorf salad sandwich and chilli chicken, the service was good with friendly staff however I will say the food is disappointing. I found the flavour quite lacking and the quality of ingredients needing improvement. I think there is potential in this place but more recipe development is needed.

name: pip's deli

comment: Amazing food and great service. The chilli chicken is absolutely incredible.

name: pip's deli

comment: Fantastic service, toasties, coffee and cookies! Definitely will be back!

name: pip's deli
address: 348 Chapel St, South Yarra VIC 3141, Australia
comment: The sandwiches here are absolutely delicious! We ordered the brisket and chicken shokupan, and both were so juicy. I personally prefer the seasoning of the brisket, and the portion of meat was really generous! The chicken shokupan has a nice Japanese sandwich vibe,the fried chicken piece is huge! It was so satisfying to eat. The highlight for me was that the Japanese-style bread was perfectly toasted and crispy, it’s the first time I’ve actually enjoyed Japanese toast this much.

name: pip's deli
address: 348 Chapel St, South Yarra VIC 3141, Australia
comment: The chicken shokupan is perfectly marinated and so tender. Paired with the marinated zucchini, 10/10!

name: pip's deli
address: 348 Chapel St, South Yarra VIC 3141, Australia
comment: Toasties were delicious and the store was so clean! Great service too. 10/10

name: pip's deli
address: 348 Chapel St, South Yarra VIC 3141, Australia
comment: We ordered the brisket melt and chilli chicken, and the cheese flavour was absolutely incredible! The beef was so fragrant and tender, a little spicy but I loved it so much. The chicken was super creamy and came with crispy fried onions – such a unique and delicious combo!
